steps.title: Identify what would prove the association
steps.content:
The conclusion is that H. ochraceiceps associates the alarm calls with danger.

The observation is that birds froze or scattered when the calls played.

But this alone doesn't prove the birds understand the calls specifically mean danger—they might just react to any loud sound.

Evidence that directly supports the conclusion would rule out this simpler explanation.

steps.title: Find which choice provides the decisive evidence
steps.content:
Choice A — When Martinez and colleagues played control sounds of random noise in the vicinity of *H. ochraceiceps*, the birds displayed no reaction. provides exactly that: a control test in which random noise produced no reaction in H. ochraceiceps, proving their response is specific to the alarm calls.

This directly supports the conclusion that they associate these calls with danger.

The other choices either describe different species, show consistency without addressing cause, or introduce a competing explanation by suggesting birds might react to the researchers.

That matches Choice A — When Martinez and colleagues played control sounds of random noise in the vicinity of *H. ochraceiceps*, the birds displayed no reaction..
